profile.name: Alikhan Beisenbayev
profile.title: Cartographer & Data Storyteller
profile.tagline: I make maps that help people see what data alone cannot.
profile.bio:
I grew up in Astana, a city still drawing itself on the steppe. I came to maps through geodesy, then stayed for the stories they can tell.

My work sits between cartography, data visualization, and the browser. I build maps for climate, cities, history, and public data — from wildfire platforms in Kazakhstan to scroll-driven narratives and printed editorial maps.

I am finishing an Erasmus Mundus MSc in Cartography, studying between Munich, Vienna, and Dresden. My thesis looks at how people engage with interactive map stories. I am interested in work where maps are not decoration, but a way to think clearly.

story.title: Born in Astana
story.description: I grew up in Kazakhstan’s capital, a city rising fast on the open steppe. Geography was never distant there. It was part of daily life.

story.title: BSc Geodesy & Cartography — ENU
story.description: I began studying geodesy and cartography at L.N. Gumilyov Eurasian National University. Projections, datums, fieldwork, and map design became the base of my practice.

story.title: GIS Intern — HERE Technologies
story.description: My first role in the mapping industry. I worked with base map data quality and built interactive web maps with Leaflet.js.

story.title: Work & Study — CSULB
story.description: I moved to California for graduate study and teaching. At the Geography department, I taught GIS to more than 100 students and built remote sensing workflows for research projects.

story.title: GIS Engineer — Armenia & Kazakhstan
story.description: I worked across two countries: building GIS infrastructure for a national geoportal in Kazakhstan and processing DEM data for a construction monitoring platform.

story.title: MSc Cartography — Semester 1, Munich
story.description: I began the Erasmus Mundus MSc in Cartography at TU München. The first semester focused on the foundations of cartography, geovisualization, and geospatial thinking.

story.title: MSc Cartography — Semester 2, Vienna
story.description: The second semester took place at TU Wien, with a focus on multimedia cartography, web mapping, mobile cartography, and location-based services.

story.title: Winner — Armenia Open Data Contest
story.description: Tigranes the Greatest, my scroll-driven map story about the Armenian Empire under Tigranes II developed with my friend Tolegen, won first place at the Armenia Open Data Contest.

story.title: Featured — GeoHipster Calendar & Atlas of Sustainability
story.description: Earth on Fire, my wildfire visualization map, was selected for the GeoHipster Calendar and featured in the Atlas of Sustainability.

story.title: Alpine Cartography Field School — Ramsau
story.description: In Ramsau am Dachstein, we worked with alpine terrain, field observation, and cartographic navigation.

story.title: Winner — British Cartographic Society
story.description: Silk Road through Centuries, my printed map of historical trade routes across Central Asia, won a British Cartographic Society competition.

story.title: MSc Cartography — Semester 3, Dresden
story.description: The third semester at TU Dresden focused on mobile cartography, 3D cartography, and advanced map communication.

story.title: MSc Thesis Defense — TU Wien
story.description: Expected thesis defense on user engagement in scroll-driven interactive map narratives about urban heat stress in European cities.
